https://github.com/coreai-microsoft/caid: Nine interviews with agent builders across enterprise and startup revealed that connecting data to agentic systems is the central pain point — developers struggle with fragmented data sources, inconsistent tooling, and unclear paths to grounding agents in trusted data. Foundry's opportunity lies in simplifying the data-to-agent pipeline, especially for knowledge retrieval and tool integration.

Interviews with 9 AI-native startup executives explored the "Day Zero" onboarding and neocloud-to-hyperscaler migration journey. Startups choose developer-first platforms like Vercel and Railway for speed, but hit scaling walls that force them toward Azure or AWS — the migration tipping point is compliance and GPU access, not feature gaps. Concept feedback on a dedicated "startup cloud" was cautiously positive, with participants wanting Azure's power without the enterprise complexity.

Agent Builder emerged as the highest-value feature and primary reason developers use the extension, but evaluation and tracing -- critical to the iterative build-test-refine loop -- are buried under Monitor instead of Build, breaking the workflow. Quick wins: re-align extension navigation to match the five-stage development lifecycle (Model Selection → Prototype → POC Validation → Deploy → Monitor), add file/CSV attachment parity with Foundry portal, and surface collaboration support for agent creation.

A survey of 27 developers plus live customer walkthroughs revealed strong preferences for how custom model training should be structured in Foundry. "Build" emerged as the clear winner for top-level navigation, while users insisted on keeping "Train" and "Fine-Tune" as distinct concepts — collapsing them causes real confusion. Buzzword-heavy labels like "AI" and "Custom" actively hurt clarity; precise terms like "Model fine-tuning" and "Task fine-tuning" are what developers actually want.

Five startup full-stack developers using Vercel, Netlify, Firebase, DigitalOcean, and Cloudflare were interviewed about how their app-building workflow is shifting from local IDE to cloud deployment toward a chat-first, browser-native paradigm. Every participant relied heavily on AI coding tools (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, ChatGPT, Gemini, V0), suggesting the IDE-to-cloud transition is being accelerated by AI-assisted development.
